differences.title: 1. RENDERING STRATEGY
differences.leftDesc: Interactive components hydrate independently, reducing initial load.
differences.rightDesc: Server-side or static generation of React components for performance.

differences.title: 2. DEFAULT OUTPUT
differences.leftDesc: Primarily generates static HTML files with minimal client-side JS.
differences.rightDesc: Can output static HTML or dynamic server-rendered pages.

differences.title: 3. CLIENT-SIDE JS
differences.leftDesc: Only interactive UI components hydrate on the client.
differences.rightDesc: React's runtime and components are sent to the client.

differences.title: 4. FRAMEWORK AGNOSTIC
differences.leftDesc: Supports React, Vue, Svelte, Solid, etc., within the same project.
differences.rightDesc: Deeply integrated with React for optimal experience.

differences.title: 5. PERFORMANCE FOCUS
differences.leftDesc: Aggressively ships zero JS to the client by default.
differences.rightDesc: Offers code splitting and performance optimizations for React.

differences.title: 6. DEVELOPMENT EXPERIENCE
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for blogs, marketing sites, and documentation.
differences.rightDesc: Suited for complex web applications needing server logic.

differences.title: 7. API ROUTES
differences.leftDesc: Basic API endpoints can be created, often serverless functions.
differences.rightDesc: Robust API routes for server-side logic within the project.
